page: Amazon Web Services r5dn.large is a memory-optimized, network and EBS-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The instance provides 2 virtual CPUs mapped to a single physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. Local storage is bundled, consisting of a 75 GB NVMe SSD, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals distinct performance tradeoffs for this instance size. While the Geekbench single-core score of 1190.0 falls into the average category, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are generally low. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is rated poor at 1736.78, and the Passmark CPU score is 1992.32. Memory bandwidth tests show 13.51 GB/sec for large block read-only operations and 11.59 GB/sec for large block write operations, both of which are in the bottom tier. Redis SET operations achieve 1.52 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput reaches 1.55 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r5dn.large offers a specialized entry point for workloads requiring high memory capacity relative to compute resources, alongside fast local NVMe storage. However, the limited compute capacity of 2 vCPUs restricts its utility for CPU-intensive tasks. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute requirements, such as small-scale caching layers, lightweight database instances, and basic data processing tasks that benefit from dedicated memory allocation and local SSD storage.
description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes a bundled 75 GB NVMe SSD and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. While its single-core Geekbench score of 1190.0 is average, its overall multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are low, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 1736.78 and a Passmark memory score of 1618.46. The instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it qualitatively cost-effective for memory-bound workloads that do not demand heavy parallel processing. It is best suited for lightweight databases, caching layers, and applications requiring fast local storage and moderate network throughput.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.large is a memory-optimized instance featuring 2 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 75 GB NVMe SSD, designed for memory-intensive workloads with moderate compute needs.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r5dn.large is a memory-optimized instance with 2 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a bundled 75 GB NVMe SSD on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring bundled NVMe SSD storage and dedicated Intel Xeon resources for memory-intensive, moderate-compute workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB DDR4 memory
Bundled 75 GB NVMe SSD local storage
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with EBS optimization
Average single-core performance paired with lower multi-core efficiency
Suited for lightweight databases, caching, and memory-bound applications
